description: Extend the shared relationship authorization plan contracts so they can represent People relationship subjects without introducing endpoint execution behavior.
acceptance-criteria: Relationship auth specs can carry person-specific subject metadata for Student, Contact, and Staff in addition to existing EducationOrganization subjects.

Person auth object metadata includes the selected auth view/table name, expected person DocumentId output column, claim EducationOrganizationId column, direct-claim-match flag set to false, and auth failure hint text.
Person subject metadata can represent direct root-column bindings, transitive ordered join paths, and zero-hop self-person root DocumentId bindings.
Stored and proposed anchor metadata are explicit enough for later GET-many and CRUD SQL builders to consume without re-resolving strategy-specific behavior.
Existing EdOrg-only relationship contract behavior, including AllowDirectClaimMatch for EdOrg hierarchy checks, remains compatible with current consumers.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationContracts.cs for People-capable contract fields and unchanged EdOrg semantics.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapter

description: Teach the shared People core to classify the five People-involved relationship strategies as supported core strategies with explicit subject-kind eligibility.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeople selects EducationOrganization, Student, Contact, and Staff subjects.
RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeopleInverted selects EducationOrganization, Student, Contact, and Staff subjects while marking only EdOrg hierarchy checks as inverted.
RelationshipsWithPeopleOnly selects Student, Contact, and Staff subjects and excludes EducationOrganization subjects.
RelationshipsWithStudentsOnly selects Student subjects only.
RelationshipsWithStudentsOnlyThroughResponsibility selects Student subjects only and records responsibility-based auth view selection.
Duplicate configured relationship strategies are preserved as separate OR entries with their raw configured indexes and relationship-local order.
NamespaceBased, OwnershipBased, custom view strategies, NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired, and invalid strategy classification behavior remain unchanged outside this People core scope.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationStrategyClassifier.cs for People strategy support and unchanged non-People classifications.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationStrategyClassifier
Add or update unit tests that assert the subject-kind matrix for all five People strategies.

description: Implement People auth view selection and missing-view configuration failure metadata from the existing auth object definitions.
acceptance-criteria: Student subjects use auth.EducationOrganizationIdToStudentDocumentId with Student_DocumentId.
Contact subjects use auth.EducationOrganizationIdToContactDocumentId with Contact_DocumentId.
Staff subjects use auth.EducationOrganizationIdToStaffDocumentId with Staff_DocumentId.
RelationshipsWithStudentsOnlyThroughResponsibility uses auth.EducationOrganizationIdToStudentDocumentIdThroughResponsibility with Student_DocumentId.
Each selected People auth view carries the matching auth.md hint text.

When a selected, operation-applicable person subject requires People auth views that were not emitted, the planning result is a security-configuration failure that names the resource, strategy, selected person kind, auth view, and all missing resources from AuthObjectDefinitions.RequiredPeopleAuthAssociationResourceNames.

Mixed EdOrg-and-People strategies that select only applicable EducationOrganization subjects continue with those EdOrg subjects even when AuthObjectDefinitions.HasAllPeopleAuthViewAssociations is false.
The planner does not emit executable specs that reference conventional People auth view names when AuthObjectDefinitions.HasAllPeopleAuthViewAssociations is false.
steps-to-verify: Review the People auth view mapping against src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/AuthObjectDefinitions.cs and AuthEdOrgHierarchyContracts.cs.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner
Review planner unit tests for all four auth view selections, hint metadata, missing-association security-configuration failures, and EdOrg-only continuation when People views are suppressed.

description: Implement People subject selection from resolved securable element paths, including direct person references, transitive person references, and self-person resources.
acceptance-criteria: Direct Student, Contact, and Staff securable elements resolve to the person DocumentId column on the subject resource root table or proposed root row.
Transitive Student, Contact, and Staff securable elements retain the ordered ResolveSecurableElementColumnPath hop chain through intermediate resource tables.
Student, Contact, and Staff resources with their own person securable element produce a zero-hop self-person subject whose terminal value is the aggregate root DocumentId.
No People subject uses UniqueId or USI values as the authorization value.
One People authorization subject is produced per independent declared securable element path, with dedupe limited to identical canonical physical predicate keys.
Contributor metadata preserves original JSON path, readable or MetaEd name, person kind, and contribution order for every contributing person securable element.
steps-to-verify: Review the new People selector against src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/AuthSecurableElementContracts.cs and the mapping set securable column path contracts.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationalPeopleAuthorizationSubjectSelector
Add unit tests covering a direct Student reference, a transitive CourseTranscript-to-Student path, Contact and Staff references, and zero-hop Student/Contact/Staff self subjects.

description: Apply ODS-parity subject scope filtering and predicate dedupe rules for People relationship subjects.
acceptance-criteria: Person securable paths whose resolved path starts from a child collection table are excluded from executable People subjects.
Excluded child-collection People paths are retained as skipped or ineligible contributor metadata with reason ChildCollectionPersonPathOutsideSubjectScope.

Identical People predicates are deduped only within a single configured strategy/check using a key that includes physical person path, value anchor, terminal person kind, auth view, and output column.
Duplicate configured strategies remain separate OR entries even when their selected People predicates are identical.
Normal and inverted mixed strategies are not deduped together, and responsibility-based Student subjects are not deduped with non-responsibility Student subjects.
If ODS subject-scope filtering leaves a selected strategy with no executable subjects, the result is a no-applicable-subject security-configuration failure that includes skipped-path diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Review the People predicate key and skipped-path metadata implementation for strategy-local scope.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationalPeopleAuthorizationSubjectSelector
Add unit tests for child-collection exclusion, same-path dedupe with merged contributors, duplicate strategy preservation, and no-applicable-subject failures.

description: Integrate selected People subjects with the existing relationship authorization planner while preserving EdOrg behavior for mixed strategies.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeople combines applicable root-table EducationOrganization subjects and applicable People subjects with AND semantics inside each strategy.
RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeopleInverted preserves the EdOrg direct-claim-match flag and swaps only the EdOrg hierarchy Source/Target filtering metadata.
Person auth view semantics are the same for normal and inverted mixed strategies.
Mixed EdOrg-and-People strategies continue with applicable People subjects when EdOrg securable elements resolve only to non-root paths.
Mixed EdOrg-and-People strategies continue with applicable EducationOrganization subjects when no selected, operation-applicable People subject requires a People auth view.
A true unresolved configured securable element remains a security-configuration failure with resource, strategy, and securable element details.
Multiple configured relationship strategies preserve OR composition metadata, raw configured indexes, and relationship-local ordering.
No endpoint adapter or SQL compiler is wired to execute People relationship checks in this task.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner.cs for combined EdOrg and People planning.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner
Add unit tests for mixed EdOrg-and-People, inverted mixed strategy metadata, People-only strategies, and unresolved element failures.

description: Model stored-value, proposed-value, and create-new eligibility for People subjects so later CRUD execution can consume the core without inventing parallel rules.
acceptance-criteria: Stored People specs carry root-table stored anchors and the ordered person path needed to resolve null or broken stored transitive paths.
Proposed People specs carry explicit proposed root-row or first-hop anchors derived from the ResourceWritePlan, with direct references represented as the degenerate single-anchor case.

Zero-hop self-person subjects are marked ineligible for POST create-new with reason SelfPersonDocumentIdUnavailableForCreateNew, while remaining applicable for stored and existing-resource operations.

When a self-person subject is ineligible for create-new and another subject in the same strategy is executable, the ineligible subject is omitted from that operation's AND check and retained in eligibility metadata.
When every subject in a selected strategy is operation-ineligible for create-new, the planner exposes a no-executable-subjects security-configuration failure with diagnostic eligibility reasons.
The core does not create a same-row People bootstrap subject for primary association resources whose proposed row would only become visible in a selected People auth view after insertion.
steps-to-verify: Review proposed anchor metadata against src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner

Add unit tests for stored transitive anchors, proposed direct and transitive anchors, self-person create-new ineligibility, mixed executable/ineligible strategy handling, and all-ineligible strategy failures.

description: Extend relationship authorization failure metadata and mapping inputs so People failures can be rendered later by AUTH1 and ProblemDetails consumers.
acceptance-criteria: People failure metadata includes person kind, selected auth view/table name, expected person DocumentId output column, value source, configured strategy identity, relationship-local order, and contributing securable element metadata.
No-claims metadata for People relationship checks identifies the selected person auth view and hint so GET-many can short-circuit to an empty result and CRUD can map to 403 later.

Stored null or broken transitive person paths are represented as stored-value invalid-data metadata for the original person securable element, not as security configuration or no-relationship metadata.
The metadata distinguishes GET-many treatment of null or broken stored person paths as nonmatching rows from CRUD stored-value invalid-data mapping for a specific resource.
Missing proposed person path values are represented as proposed element-required metadata for the original person securable element.
The existing versioned compact AUTH1 failure-set ordinal model can map People strategy and subject ordinals back to the extended metadata without adding user-readable values to the payload.
Security-configuration failures for no applicable People subjects include resource, strategy, selected subject kinds, and skipped or unresolved securable element details.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload.cs and RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper.cs for People metadata compatibility.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper
Add unit tests for People no-claims, stored invalid-data metadata, proposed missing metadata, auth-view hints, and ordinal-to-metadata mapping.

description: Preserve the existing staged runtime behavior for GET-many and CRUD adapters until DMS-1095 and DMS-1158 consume the People core.
acceptance-criteria: Existing GET-many adapters do not expose or execute People relationship specs before the DMS-1095 integration story.
Existing GET-by-id, POST, PUT, and DELETE adapters do not expose or execute People relationship specs before the DMS-1158 integration story.
Known-but-not-enabled or explicit staging behavior remains in place for endpoint paths that are not part of this core story.
EdOrg-only GET-many and CRUD authorization behavior remains unchanged.
No E2E feature tags are re-enabled as part of this core story.
No production DDL, auth view definitions, or database execution paths are changed except where needed to inspect existing auth object metadata.
steps-to-verify: Review PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapter, SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler, and CRUD orchestrators for unchanged People execution staging.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapter
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor
Search the E2E feature files to confirm no @relational-backend tags were re-enabled for People scenarios in this story.

description: Add focused unit coverage for the People relationship core and run the scoped formatting and test pass for the implementation.
acceptance-criteria: Unit tests cover strategy classification for all five People relationship strategies.

Unit tests cover subject-kind selection, auth view selection, inverted EdOrg metadata, transitive person path handling, zero-hop self subjects, responsibility-based Student handling, and no-subject security failures.
Unit tests cover missing People auth view guard behavior, child-collection path exclusion, operation eligibility reasons, contributor metadata, and duplicate strategy preservation.
Existing EdOrg-only planner, adapter, AUTH1 payload, and failure mapper tests still pass.
Changed C# files under the DMS backend and backend unit test projects are formatted with csharpier.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor
Run git diff -- tasks.json and confirm no production or test source files were edited by the task-refinement pass itself.
